01 — About

Engineer by training,
AI practitioner by choice.

I graduated 1st in my batch in Electrical Power Engineering from Al-Balqa Applied University (GPA 3.63). My background spans electrical systems, embedded systems, and robotics — evolved into a focus on intelligent automation and AI-driven solutions.

I work as an Electrical Engineer, Crestron Programmer, and Automation Engineer — designing, programming, and commissioning large-scale AV and control systems. I build VT-Pro touch panel interfaces and write SIMPL/SIMPL+ code for Crestron processors. I have delivered projects in both Jordan and Saudi Arabia, including the Al Qaswa Interactive Experience in KSA.

In parallel, I'm advancing my applied AI skills through the AI.SPIRE program — building ML pipelines, NLP systems, and production-grade data infrastructure. My goal: bridge physical automation with intelligent AI software.

04 — Crestron Systems

Programming automation
at professional scale.

At Sustainable Smart Cities Co., I programmed and commissioned Crestron control systems for large-scale AV installations in Saudi Arabia. This included building VT-Pro touch panel interfaces, writing SIMPL and SIMPL+ code, and integrating all AV components. Screenshots below are from the Al Qaswa Interactive Experience — per the official installation reports dated December 14–15, 2025.

Portfolio / Real-World Projects / NASA Space Apps
🚀 NASA Space Apps Challenge 2023 — Jordan

Jordan's Star
STAR Project

"Revolutionizing Technical Standards with AI" — a project built for the NASA Space Apps Challenge 2023. We developed an AI-driven system to analyze and improve technical standards documentation. Placed 2nd nationally in Jordan.

🏆 2nd Place — Jordan NASA Space Apps 2023 AI / NLP Standards Analysis Oct 2023
🛸
2ND PLACE · JORDAN · NASA SPACE APPS 2023
🥈
2nd Place — NASA Space Apps Challenge Jordan 2023 Competed among teams from across Jordan in NASA's global innovation hackathon
The Challenge

What we were asked to solve

The NASA Space Apps Challenge is a global hackathon held simultaneously in cities worldwide. In 2023, our team in Jordan built a solution for the "STAR: Revolutionizing Technical Standards" challenge — developing tools that help organizations navigate, interpret, and apply complex technical standards documentation.

Our Solution

AI-driven standards intelligence

We built an AI-powered system that ingests technical standards documents and makes them searchable and queryable using natural language. Engineers could ask questions about standards in plain language and receive relevant sections, context, and related requirements.

NLP Pipeline
Text extraction, processing, and semantic indexing of technical standards documentation.
AI Query Engine
Natural language interface — ask questions, get precise document sections back.
Cross-Reference
Automatic identification of related clauses and dependencies within standards.
Timeline

From brief to 2nd place in 48 hours

DAY 0 — Challenge Selection
Team formed, challenge brief analyzed. Identified the core problem: technical standards are inaccessible and hard to cross-reference.
DAY 1 — Build Sprint
Built the NLP pipeline for document ingestion, developed the query interface, and implemented cross-reference detection logic.
DAY 2 — Demo & Presentation
Finalized the demo, prepared the presentation, and rehearsed the pitch for judges.
JUDGING — 2nd Place Nationally
Presented to NASA Space Apps judges. Placed 2nd in Jordan — competing against teams from universities and companies across the country.
Reflection

What this meant for me

This was one of my first projects applying AI to a real-world domain problem under a 48-hour constraint. It sparked my interest in NLP and retrieval systems — topics I'm now studying formally in the AI.SPIRE program through RAG and knowledge graph modules.

This project directly inspired my current AI.SPIRE focus on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and knowledge graph systems — the same core ideas, now applied with much deeper technical depth.
